subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The elements in a period show trends in physical properties like atomic radius and electronegativity.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is due to increase in effective nuclear charge with increase in atomic number.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The effective nuclear charge increases with increase in atomic number, which leads to decrease in atomic radius and increase in electronegativity in a period.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The elements in a group show trends in physical properties like atomic radius and electronegativity.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is due to increase in atomic number with each successive element in a group.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Although atomic number increases in a group, atomic radius decreases and electronegativity increases, but not due to increase in atomic number alone, but due to the increase in effective nuclear charge.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The elements in the same period show similar chemical properties.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is due to the same number of electron shells in the atoms of these elements.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The elements in the same period show similar chemical properties due to the same number of electron shells in the atoms of these elements, resulting in similar valence shell electron configuration.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The elements in the same group show similar chemical properties.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is due to the same number of valence electrons in the atoms of these elements.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The elements in the same group show similar chemical properties due to the same number of valence electrons in the atoms of these elements, resulting in similar chemical behavior.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The elements in the same period do not show similar physical properties.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is due to the increase in atomic number with each successive element in a period.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The elements in the same period do show similar physical properties, not due to increase in atomic number, but due to the same number of electron shells in the atoms of these elements.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The elements in Group 1 show similar chemical properties.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is due to the presence of only one valence electron in the atoms of these elements.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The elements in Group 1 show similar chemical properties due to the presence of only one valence electron in the atoms of these elements, resulting in similar chemical behavior.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The elements in the same group show similar physical properties.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is due to the same number of energy levels in the atoms of these elements.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The elements in the same group show similar chemical properties, not physical properties, due to the same number of valence electrons in the atoms of these elements.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The elements in Group 17 show similar chemical properties.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is due to the presence of seven valence electrons in the atoms of these elements.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The elements in Group 17 show similar chemical properties due to the presence of seven valence electrons in the atoms of these elements, resulting in similar chemical behavior.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The atomic radius of the elements in a period decreases from left to right.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is due to the increase in effective nuclear charge with increase in atomic number.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The atomic radius of the elements in a period decreases from left to right due to the increase in effective nuclear charge with increase in atomic number.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The elements in the same group show similar chemical properties due to the same number of energy levels in the atoms of these elements.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The elements in the same group show similar chemical properties due to the same number of valence electrons in the atoms of these elements, not due to the same number of energy levels.
